title: Zillow Q4 Revenue Up Again in Q4 2023, but Annual Net Loss Persists
text: Zillow Q4 2023 Earnings Analysis. Financial Highlights: Revenue Performance: Zillow reported Q4 2023 revenue of $474 million, representing 9% growth year-over-year. However, full-year 2023 revenue declined to $1.5 billion, down 5% compared to 2022. Net Loss: The company posted a $73 million net loss in Q4 2023, matching the prior-year quarter's loss. For the full year, Zillow's net loss reached $158 million, nearly doubling the $101 million loss recorded in 2022. Business Segment Performance: Residential Revenue: Grew 3% year-over-year to $349 million in Q4, outperforming the broader industry's 4% transaction value decline. Rentals Revenue: Demonstrated strong momentum with $93 million in revenue, up 37% year-over-year. Multifamily revenue surged 52% year-over-year. Mortgage Revenue: Increased 22% year-over-year to $22 million, driven by a 105% increase in purchase loan origination volume. CEO Outlook: CEO Rich Barton characterized the results positively, emphasizing Zillow's market leadership and plans to expand integrated customer experiences to additional markets in 2024. He also highlighted the company's position outside current industry litigation. Market Position: Zillow maintains its standing as the leading real estate portal with significant rental platform dominance and strong brand recognition.
